title: Internal Developer Platform (IDP)
summary: Building an IDP with Backstage, golden paths, self-service provisioning, and reducing developer cognitive load.

sections.content:
## What is an Internal Developer Platform?

An Internal Developer Platform (IDP) is a self-service layer built on top of your infrastructure that lets application developers create, deploy, and manage services without deep infrastructure expertise. It codifies platform best practices into reusable, discoverable tooling.

**What a good IDP provides:**
- Service templates (create a new service in minutes)
- Self-service environment provisioning
- Unified service catalog with ownership
- Pre-configured CI/CD pipelines
- Observability dashboards out of the box
- Secrets management integration

sections.title: IDP Layered Architecture
sections.content:
graph TB
    Dev[Developer] --> Portal[Developer Portal\nBackstage]
    Portal --> Catalog[Service Catalog]
    Portal --> Templates[Software Templates]
    Portal --> Workflows[Self-Service Workflows]
    Workflows --> CI[CI/CD\nGitHub Actions]
    Workflows --> Infra[Infra Provisioning\nTerraform + Crossplane]
    Workflows --> K8s[K8s Deployment\nHelm + ArgoCD]
    Catalog --> Docs[TechDocs]
    Catalog --> Deps[Dependency Graph]
    Catalog --> OnCall[On-Call Info]
    style Portal fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Dev fill:#059669,color:#fff

## Backstage — The Foundation

Backstage (Spotify, now CNCF) is the most widely adopted open-source IDP framework.

```yaml
# catalog-info.yaml — every service has this
apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: order-service
  description: Manages the order lifecycle
  tags: [java, spring-boot, critical]
  annotations:
    github.com/project-slug: org/order-service
    backstage.io/techdocs-ref: dir:.
    pagerduty.com/service-id: P123ABC
    grafana/dashboard-selector: "service=order-service"
spec:
  type: service
  lifecycle: production
  owner: group:team-orders
  system: ecommerce-platform
  dependsOn:
    - component:payment-service
    - resource:orders-postgresql
    - resource:orders-kafka-topic
  providesApis:
    - order-api-v2
```

Backstage aggregates this across all repos and gives you a single pane of glass.

## Software Templates

Templates let developers scaffold new services from proven blueprints:

```yaml
# template.yaml
apiVersion: scaffolder.backstage.io/v1beta3
kind: Template
metadata:
  name: spring-boot-service
  title: Spring Boot Microservice
spec:
  parameters:
    - title: Service Details
      properties:
        serviceName:
          type: string
          description: Name of the service (kebab-case)
        owner:
          type: string
          ui:field: OwnerPicker
  steps:
    - id: fetch-template
      action: fetch:template
      input:
        url: ./skeleton   # git repo with template code
        values:
          serviceName: ${{ parameters.serviceName }}
    - id: publish
      action: publish:github
      input:
        repoUrl: github.com?owner=org&repo=${{ parameters.serviceName }}
    - id: register
      action: catalog:register  # auto-register in catalog
```

Result: developer fills a form → gets a GitHub repo with Spring Boot app, Dockerfile, GitHub Actions CI, Helm chart, Grafana dashboard, and catalog entry — all pre-configured.

sections.title: Golden Path — From Idea to Production
sections.content:
graph LR
    Dev[Developer] -->|fills form| Template[Backstage Template]
    Template -->|creates| Repo[GitHub Repo\nSpring Boot skeleton]
    Repo -->|triggers| CI[CI Pipeline\nBuild, Test, Scan]
    CI -->|on merge to main| CD[ArgoCD\nDeploy to staging]
    CD -->|auto-configured| Grafana[Grafana Dashboard]
    CD -->|auto-configured| Alerts[PagerDuty Alerts]
    Dev -->|manual approval| Prod[Production Deploy]
    style Template fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Prod fill:#059669,color:#fff

## Measuring IDP Success

### DORA Metrics (DevOps Research and Assessment)
- **Deployment Frequency** — how often you deploy to production
- **Lead Time for Changes** — commit to production time
- **Change Failure Rate** — % deployments that cause incidents
- **Mean Time to Restore (MTTR)** — time to recover from incident

### IDP-specific metrics:
- Time to first deployment for new service
- % services using golden paths
- Developer NPS / satisfaction surveys
- Number of platform tickets vs self-service completions

rows.Description: Shell scripts, cookiecutter, shared GitHub Actions
Service catalog, basic templates, CI/CD integration
Cost insights, automated remediation, AI assistance

sections.question: What is the primary metric that indicates an IDP is successful?
options: Number of services registered in the catalog
Reduction in platform team ticket volume and improvement in developer DORA metrics
sections.explanation: A successful IDP moves developers from filing tickets to self-service. Key signals: platform ticket volume decreases, DORA metrics improve (faster deployments, lower lead time), and developer satisfaction scores increase. The catalog size or plugin count are vanity metrics — what matters is whether developers can ship faster with fewer blockers.
